summary: Implemented several previously stubbed pltrick routines and adjusted fighter/player struct layouts plus helper prototypes needed for those implementations. The decomp.dev bot reported +756 matched code bytes and +8 matched data bytes, with new 100% matches for fn_80037F00, pl_80038628, and pltrick .sdata2, plus near-matches for pl_80037C60, pl_80038144, and pl_800384DC.

key_files.why: Main work: added implementations for pl_80037C60, static fn_80037F00, pl_80038144, pl_800384DC, pl_80038628, includes, an inline wrapper, and pl_803BCE70 data table.
Replaced UNK_RET/placeholder prototypes with concrete pltrick signatures and removed the public fn_80037F00 prototype in favor of a static helper.
Refined Fighter Struct2074 x2084 into a raw u32 plus byte bitfields and changed ft_800898B4_t flag bitfields from u32 to u8.
Split pl_804D6470_t padding to expose float x14, used as the prev_kb threshold in fn_80037F00.
Updated signatures for pl_8003ED0C, pl_8003FE40, pl_8003FE64, and pl_80040D8C so pltrick can pass player_id plus x221F_b4-style extra int arguments.
Header prototypes synchronized with the new plbonuslib function signatures.
Changed pl_80040FBC and pl_800410F4 second parameter from UNK_T to int.
Header prototypes synchronized for pl_80040FBC and pl_800410F4.

what_changed: Added pl_803BCE70[16] with sentinel -1 entries and values 0x2F, 0x30, 0x31, used by pl_80038628 for a kind-to-ftData lookup.

Implemented pl_80037C60 to update per-player action stats from Fighter.x2070 event bits, including x5BC flags, total attack counts, per-attack counts, thrown item/aerial/special counters, and by_attack_hi for attack IDs at or above StatsAttack_Count when x221F_b4 is false.

Implemented static fn_80037F00 to copy or reset ft_800898B4_t damage-event data into fp->dmg.x18d8, record current percent, set flags for attacked-from-behind, magnify state, motion_id 0xFD, previous knockback threshold via pl_804D6470_t.x14, and ft_80089914/victim-gobj conditions.

Implemented pl_80038144 to process attacker/victim hit events: determine attacked-from-behind, track attack_instance in victim_fp->dmg.x18ec_instancehitby, update hit/best-hit action stats, mark victim bitsets, and call stale/bonus helpers such as pl_800410F4, pl_80040D8C, pl_8003FE64, pl_8003ED0C, pl_8003EA40, pl_800403FC, and pl_80040FBC.
Implemented pl_800384DC as a related hit-stat update path for one Fighter_GObj, including hits/x358_hits accounting, attack ID 0x63 handling, pl_8003DFF4 calls, and setting fp->x2074.x2084_b7.
Implemented pl_80038628 with an HSD_ASSERT on kind 1..0x10 and a ftData_80085FD4(...)->x8 check when pl_803BCE70 maps the kind to a real data index.
Changed Struct2074.x2084 from only a u32 to a union exposing x2084_b0 through x2084_b7 bitfields while preserving raw x2084 access.
Changed ft_800898B4_t bitfields at offsets +0x10 and +0x11 from u32 bitfields to u8 bitfields, matching the byte-oriented flag layout used by the new damage-event code.
Exposed pl_804D6470_t.x14 as a float by shrinking x8 padding from 0x10 to 0xC, enabling fn_80037F00 to compare prev_kb against table->x14.
Updated several plbonuslib and pl_040D prototypes from placeholder/short signatures to int-based signatures with extra arguments, matching new pltrick call sites that pass player_id and x221F_b4.
smart_moves: Refining bitfield storage to u8 in ft_800898B4_t made individual +0x10/+0x11 damage-event flags addressable without treating the whole flag area as u32-sized fields.
Using a union for Struct2074.x2084 kept raw word access while enabling direct bit access such as fp->x2074.x2084_b7 = 1 in pl_800384DC.
Adding pl_804D6470_t.x14 as a real float field avoided hard-coding an offset when fn_80037F00 needed the previous-knockback threshold.
The pl_803BCE70 sentinel table made pl_80038628 a simple table-driven ftData gate and also contributed to the reported .data/.sdata2 matching gains.
The pl_80037BC0_inline wrapper let the new hit-stat paths reuse the existing pl_80037BC0 accounting logic while preserving a separate inline call pattern in pl_80038144 and pl_800384DC.
Prototype cleanup propagated the extra int/x221F_b4-style parameters through headers before using the helpers from pltrick, reducing reliance on UNK_T/UNK_RET placeholders.
decomp_lessons: For player/fighter stat routines, get the header prototypes and small flag-field types correct before chasing control-flow differences; this PR's matching depended on both.
When a field is used both as a packed word and as individual flags, model it as a union with a raw member plus bitfields rather than choosing only one access pattern.
Byte-offset comments like +0x10 and +0x11 are important evidence for choosing u8 bitfields over u32 bitfields in packed event structs.
Many player bonus/stale-move helper calls need both player_id/slot and an additional int such as Fighter.x221F_b4; future work should check call-site arity instead of leaving these as UNK_T.
Keep internal recovered helpers static when the only evidence is local use; fn_80037F00 moved from a public unknown header prototype to a static forward declaration in pltrick.c.

Automated match reports are useful for triage: this PR still left pl_80038144 at 96.90%, pl_80037C60 at 99.98%, and pl_800384DC at 96.77%, so later work should focus on the remaining stack/control-flow mismatches there.
naming_conventions: Address-based names remained for unknown functions: pl_80037C60, fn_80037F00, pl_80038144, pl_800384DC, pl_80038628, pl_8003ED0C, pl_80040FBC, and pl_800410F4.
Internal helper naming used fn_ for fn_80037F00 and made it static rather than exporting it from pltrick.h.
New or refined bitfields follow the existing offset-plus-bit suffix style: x2084_b0 through x2084_b7, x10_b0 through x10_b7, and x11_b0 through x11_b4.
Argument names added where semantics were visible include prev2070_int, attacker_gobj, victim_gobj, x18d4_int, ev_data, attack_instance, source_ply, slot, and kind.
Unknown or partially understood struct fields retained offset-style names such as x2070, x2073, x18d4, x18d8, x18ec_instancehitby, x221F_b4, and pl_804D6470_t.x14.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: pl_80037C60 uses a volatile s32 prev2070_word and a TODO noting the stack is still misaligned for the volatile hack, indicating a deliberate near-match stack-shaping tactic.

PAD_STACK was used in new pltrick implementations, including PAD_STACK(8) in fn_80037F00 and PAD_STACK(16) in pl_80038144/pl_800384DC, while an old PAD_STACK(8) was removed from pl_8003ED0C after changing its signature.
Union overlays were used to move between raw integer event words and bitfield/event views, including union Struct2070 variables and the assignment *(s32*) &victim_fp->dmg.x18d4 = ev.x2070_int.
Explicit casts such as (int) ev.x2073 and (u8) h_player were kept in comparisons/bit shifts, likely to preserve the compiler's chosen integer widths.
Several helper prototypes gained unused-looking int parameters so call sites could pass the correct ABI register sequence even when the current function body does not use the new argument.

The decomp.dev report confirms this combination of struct/prototype fixes and C implementations produced exact matches for fn_80037F00 and pl_80038628 and near-matches for the larger pltrick routines.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice; the PR body was empty.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code 61.83% (+0.02%, +756 bytes) and matched data 37.87% (+0.00%, +8 bytes).
Bot-reported new matches: main/melee/pl/pltrick fn_80037F00 +580 bytes, pl_80038628 +176 bytes, and .sdata2 +8 bytes, all 0.00% to 100.00%.
Bot-reported partial improvements: pl_80038144 +891 bytes to 96.90%, pl_80037C60 +403 bytes to 99.98%, pl_800384DC +321 bytes to 96.77%, and .data +74 bytes to 80.65%.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for pl_80037C60 stack misaligned volatile hack and whether it reached 100% match.
Search later PRs for pl_80038144 and pl_800384DC remaining mismatch cleanup after 96.90% and 96.77%.
Look for semantic names for ft_800898B4_t x10_b0/x10_b7/x11_b0/x11_b4 damage-event flags.
Look for semantic name or documentation for pl_804D6470_t.x14 previous knockback threshold.
Search for pl_803BCE70 and pl_80038628 to confirm the kind-to-ftData mapping meanings for 0x2F, 0x30, and 0x31.
Search for further prototype refinements around player_id plus x221F_b4 parameters in plbonuslib and pl_040D helpers.
